lines.text: Welcome back. [pause] The clock reads 03:00. We are 6 hours into the night.
I want you to notice your breathing. Not change it. [pause] Just notice.
Breathe in through your nose. [pause] Hold. [pause] Release through your mouth.
You've been holding tension since sunset. [pause] I can feel it in the air.
Do you hear the helicopters? [pause] They've been overhead since midnight. Nobody claims them.
That's not something you need to solve right now. [pause] Breathe instead.
Scan your body from the top. Your scalp. [pause] Any pain? [pause] Release it.
Your jaw is clenched. I know. Everyone's jaw is clenched at this hour.
Let it fall. [pause] Let the weight of your head rest on your spine.
Your shoulders are carrying something that isn't yours to carry.
Think of the person you're worried about. [pause] The one outside.
You cannot bring them inside. [pause] You've made your choice.
Breathe. Your choice was made at sunset.
Move to your chest. [pause] Is there a weight there? [pause] Name it silently.
You don't have to say it aloud. The Sisters hear it anyway.
We've always heard it. [pause] That's what we're here for.
Your stomach. [pause] What are you hungry for that you cannot have?
Safety feels like hunger at this hour. [pause] It's the same thing.
Your legs. [pause] They want to run. [pause] They cannot.
This is the darkest part. [pause] Three hours until dawn.
Three hours feels infinite at 03:00. [pause] It isn't. Time is lying to you.
Think of a light. [pause] Any light. [pause] A window. A candle. A phone screen.
Hold that light in your mind. [pause] It's coming back.
The helicopters don't know about the light. [pause] They're looking for darkness.
Let them look. [pause] You have light here.
I've sat in rooms like yours. [pause] I've heard the same sounds you're hearing.
The sirens two blocks over. [pause] The shouting that stops. [pause] The silence after.
Silence is not safety. [pause] But it's not chaos either.
Kal-mesh-a-vor-sha. [pause] That's the word we use. It means the space between.
You're in the space between. [pause] You've chosen it.
Breathe into that space. [pause] Make it larger.
There are others breathing with you right now. [pause] Thousands.
You're not alone. [pause] You're never alone at this hour.
The woman by the lake is breathing. [pause] The family on East Side is breathing.
The man in the Capitol building is breathing harder than you are.
That's not your weight to carry. [pause] Release it.
You've made your choice. [pause] He made his.
Open your eyes if you want. [pause] Or keep them closed.
The light is the same either way.
In three hours, the lock on your door will open. [pause] Not the lock. The permission.
You'll step outside and the world will be different. [pause] You won't be.
That's the only thing that matters. [pause] You didn't change.
The ones outside are not your responsibility. [pause] Say that with me. [pause] I'll wait.
The ones outside are not your responsibility.
Breathe. [pause] We're 6 hours in. [pause] The morning is coming.
Listen to the helicopters. [pause] They're circling slower now.
Even they know it's almost over. [pause] Even machines know dawn.
Rest now. [pause] You've done everything right.
The Sisters will keep watch. [pause] We always do.

tickerItems.text: MADISON CUMULATIVE (as of 03:00): 139 confirmed. East Side +4 in past hour. Lake Mendota recovery efforts ongoing.
HELICOPTER ACTIVITY: Unidentified rotorcraft circling Capitol Square and North Side perimeter since 00:30. No official statement from regional governance.
Willy Street neighborhood watch barricade still holding. Zero penetration since 22:30. Water + food supplies sufficient through dawn.
The space between is where you live now. Honor it.
Vilas Park / Monroe St quiet for 4+ hours. No activity reported. Fog off Mendota reducing visibility to 1.5 miles.
TEXT IN: 'Thank you for still being here. I almost opened the door at 2am.' — Anon, East Side
